text:
the Sage
Vrddha (Senior) Garga, the movements of the Seven Seers (the
Great Bear), through whom the northern region shines as though
bedecked with a pearl necklace, like a maiden with a smiling coun-
tenance wearing a garland of white lotuses, by whom she appears to
have a lord (or husband), and by whose circular movements the
northern quarter seems verily dancing at the instruction of the
leader, viz., the Pole-Star.
[The author who is a born poet cannot resist the overtures of
his muse and bursts into fine poetry at the sight of the beautiful line
of seven stars looking like a pearl necklace which he must have en-
joyed on several nights. He employs in this verse simile, metaphor
and poetic fancy.
Cf. —

The Seven Sages were stationed in the asterism Magha when
king Yudhisthira was ruling over the earth. The commencement
of the Saka era took place 2526 years after the period of that
monarch.
